nysed-ela-2013-g8-mc-q1.alt: Question 1. Based on the entire passage, what is the meaning of the word “momentous” in line 18? Choices: A: causes much happiness B: creates a great disturbance C: occurs simply by chance D: becomes historically important

nysed-ela-2013-g8-mc-q2.alt: Question 2. Read the sentence from line 12 of the passage. People were running toward him like iron filings to a magnet. The author uses this simile to emphasize that the people Choices: A: were interested in the news about the gold B: were curious about the ship’s arrival C: wanted to become gold prospectors D: were unable to resist reading about the gold

nysed-ela-2013-g8-mc-q3.alt: Question 3. Why does the author alternate between sharing information from the newspaper and showing Jason’s response? Choices: A: to suggest why Jason misses his brothers B: to show Jason’s feelings at the moment they occur C: to portray Jason’s interest in reading D: to show why Jason might want to return home

nysed-ela-2013-g8-mc-q4.alt: Question 4. Which sentence from the passage best supports the conclusion that Jason is fascinated by the story of the discovery? Choices: A: “It was unbelievable, all this pushing and shoving.” (line 21) B: “When there was only one left, Jason took off running with it like a dog with a prize bone.” (lines 25 and 26) C: “That man had left home with almost nothing to his name, Jason thought, just like I did.” (lines 55 and 56) D: “It could have been Jason Hawthorn dragging a fortune in gold off that ship.” (line 57)

nysed-ela-2013-g8-mc-q5.alt: Question 5. Which detail would be most important to include in a summary of the passage? Choices: A: Jason had gone to New York instead of Alaska. B: The headline in the newspaper is written in large print. C: A boarding house is near the dock in Seattle. D: Jason’s brothers work at a sawmill.

nysed-ela-2013-g8-mc-q6.alt: Question 6. As mentioned in line 16, Jason wants to shout “Seattle is where I’m from” to show that Choices: A: he has already heard this news B: he knows why the crowd is excited C: he knows the prospectors on the ship D: he has a personal connection to the events

nysed-ela-2013-g8-mc-q7.alt: Question 7. Read the excerpt from lines 63 through 65 of the passage. A nation unrecovered from the panic of’93 and four years of depression now casts its hopeful eyes upon Alaska. Today’s events, in a lightning stroke, point north from Seattle toward that vast and ultimate frontier whose riches have only begun to be plumbed. What do these sentences mainly suggest? Choices: A: that Alaska was an exciting state B: that many Americans desired great wealth C: that the country was still a developing nation D: that a great discovery was inspiring the country
